top_signals.title: China’s Crude Imports Set to Hit Weakest Level Since 2016
top_signals.summary: Chinese crude oil imports this month are on course to book an even weaker month than May, according to Kpler data, which sees the daily average at just 6.4 million barrels, as cited by Bloomberg. Data from Vortexa suggests a similar daily import level, the publication added. This would be the weakest import rate since October 2016, Bloomberg noted, and an 8% decline on May volumes. Those averaged 7.82 million barrels, according to customs data, down by 29% on the year and 17% on April. The May average was also a 38% drop on February volumes, before…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.summary: Competition watchdog examining whether group implemented ‘misleading’ campaign against rival flu vaccine maker

top_signals.title: ON Semiconductor strikes $7 billion deal for Synaptics in physical AI push
top_signals.summary: ON Semiconductor said the deal bumps up its total addressable market by $30 billion, to $243 billion by 2030.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Novak Djokovic Joins General Atlantic in His Wall Street Debut
top_signals.summary: Novak Djokovic, a 24-time Grand Slam tennis champion, joined General Atlantic as the private equity firm dives deeper into sports investing. “This is the first partnership of this kind for me,” Djokovic told Bloomberg. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Commissioner Tzitzikostas delivers a Speech at the "Prague–Berlin–Copenhagen: First EU pilot project for new international rail connections"

top_signals.title: Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile
top_signals.summary: Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing you to manage long-running Claude tasks from your phone. [...]

top_signals.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
top_signals.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

alerts.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
alerts.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
